steps.title: Use the confidence clue
steps.content: The statistical analysis confirmed the signal at over 99% confidence. That means the analysis helped rule out uncertainty about whether the chirping sound was truly evidence of gravitational waves.

steps.title: Reject unrelated meanings
steps.content: Choice A means hard to notice, but the issue is interpretation, not visibility. Choice B means tact or freedom of choice. Choice D names probability, which the analysis established rather than precluded.

steps.title: Name the uncertainty
steps.content: Ambiguity is uncertainty or openness to multiple interpretations, so ruling out claims of ambiguity fits the confirmed signal. The answer is C.
